codeReview.summary: Comprehensive D1-based sliding window rate limiting implementation covering all three required endpoint types. The implementation uses atomic D1 UPDATE operations for counter increments, returns 429 with Retry-After headers when limits are exceeded, and properly isolates rate limits between different identifiers (IPs, tokens, slugs).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rate limiting implementation follows the established patterns in AGENTS.md: standardized error shape with { error, code, retry_after }, proper TypeScript strict mode, atomic D1 operations, and comprehensive test coverage.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker/src/middleware/rate-limit.ts:79-86 returns standardized error with RATE_LIMIT code and retry_after field matching the API Error Codes convention in AGENTS.md

sharedStateObservations.observation: The sliding window implementation correctly handles window expiration by checking if now >= windowEnd before allowing requests, then resetting the window. This is a subtle but important detail that prevents the 'thundering herd' problem at window boundaries.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker/src/middleware/rate-limit.ts:68-72 and 107-113 show proper window expiration handling - returns null (allow) when window expired, then creates new window on recordRateLimit

sharedStateObservations.observation: IP extraction correctly prioritizes CF-Connecting-IP header (Cloudflare-specific) over X-Forwarded-For, with a fallback to 'unknown' for local development. This ensures the real client IP is used even behind Cloudflare's proxy.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker/src/middleware/rate-limit.ts:20-37 implements getClientIP with CF-Connecting-IP priority, matching Cloudflare Workers best practices

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the worker-backend skill procedure: created migration (004_rate_limits.sql), updated schema.sql, wrote tests first (TDD red phase), then implemented the feature. Handoff confirms followedProcedure: true with no deviations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff shows tests created before implementation: test/rate-limiting.test.ts created with failing tests, then implementation added to make them pass

summary: The rate-limiting-d1 feature is fully implemented and meets all requirements. The D1-based sliding window approach uses atomic UPDATE operations for counter increments, properly isolates limits between identifiers (IP addresses for POST /spikes, bearer tokens for POST /shares, and slug+IP combinations for password attempts), and returns 429 responses with accurate Retry-After headers. All 13 new rate-limiting tests pass alongside the existing 88 tests (101 total), with zero TypeScript errors and zero lint errors. The implementation correctly handles edge cases including window expiration, different IPs having independent limits, different shares having independent password attempt limits, and different bearer tokens having independent share creation limits.
